title: Iger Has Told Associates He Plans to Leave CEO Role Before Contract Expires
title_cn: 艾格已告知同事他计划在合同到期前卸任CEO职务

paragraphs.cn: 2026年1月30日下午4:47（美国东部时间）

paragraphs.en:
Disney DIS 1.09%
increase; green up pointing triangle
 Chief Executive Bob Iger has told associates that he plans to step down as CEO and pull back from daily management before the Dec. 31 end of his contract, according to people familiar with the matter.
paragraphs.cn: 据知情人士透露，迪士尼（DIS 1.09%上涨；绿色向上箭头）首席执行官鲍勃·艾格已告知同事，他计划在合同于12月31日到期前辞去CEO职务，并退出日常管理。

paragraphs.en: The entertainment giant’s board of directors is planning to meet next week at its headquarters in Burbank, Calif., where they are expected to vote on who should take the top job, according to people familiar with the matter.
paragraphs.cn: 据知情人士透露，这家娱乐巨头的董事会计划下周在加利福尼亚州伯班克的总部召开会议，预计届时将投票决定谁将接任最高职位。

paragraphs.en: In private conversations over the last few months, Iger has told people close to him that he is ready to move on from the grind of being CEO and was frustrated by conflicts at Disney’s ABC network over the brief suspension of late night host Jimmy Kimmel, people who have spoken with him said.
paragraphs.cn: 与艾格交谈过的人士表示，在过去几个月的私人对话中，艾格告诉亲近他的人，他已准备好摆脱担任CEO的繁重工作，并对迪士尼ABC电视网因深夜节目主持人吉米·坎摩尔短暂停职而引发的冲突感到沮丧。

paragraphs.en: The CEO has told multiple associates that he would like to spend more of his time and energy on other things, such as sailing his new and larger superyacht, the Aquarius—which was completed and delivered to him last summer. He has said he would also like to devote more time to work with his wife, Willow Bay, dean of the USC Annenberg School for Communication and Journalism, and on Angel City FC, the women’s soccer team they bought in 2024.
paragraphs.cn: 这位CEO已告知多位同事，他希望将更多时间和精力投入到其他事情上，例如驾驶他去年夏天完工并交付的全新大型超级游艇“水瓶座”号。他还表示，希望投入更多时间与妻子、南加州大学安纳伯格传播与新闻学院院长柳·贝（Willow Bay）一起工作，并参与他们于2024年收购的女子足球队天使城FC的事务。

paragraphs.en: The final timing of his departure from the top job hasn’t yet been determined and could change. Iger is expected to remain CEO for several months after the company announces who will succeed him so that he can mentor the new leader, people with knowledge of his plans said. He could retain a role on the board and at the company after the CEO transition.
paragraphs.cn: 他卸任最高职位的最终时间尚未确定，并可能发生变化。据了解他计划的人士称，艾格预计将在公司宣布继任者后继续担任CEO数月，以便指导新领导人。在CEO过渡后，他可能会在董事会和公司中保留一个职位。

paragraphs.en: People close to the company believe the CEO race has come down to two candidates: Experiences chairman Josh D’Amaro, who oversees theme parks, consumer products and videogames, and Dana Walden, a veteran television executive who co-chairs Disney’s entertainment business, including streaming. Both executives presented their visions for the company’s future to the board in August, The Wall Street Journal previously reported. Many believe D’Amaro is the front-runner.
paragraphs.cn: 接近公司的人士认为，CEO之争已归结为两名候选人：负责主题公园、消费产品和视频游戏的体验部门主席乔什·达马罗（Josh D’Amaro），以及共同领导迪士尼娱乐业务（包括流媒体）的资深电视高管达娜·瓦尔登（Dana Walden）。《华尔街日报》此前报道称，两位高管均于八月向董事会阐述了他们对公司未来的愿景。许多人认为达马罗是领跑者。

paragraphs.en: He told one associate that he wanted to give his successor a “fresh start” and that lingering until the end of the year would hinder whomever the board picks to lead the company going forward.
paragraphs.cn: 他告诉一位同事，他希望给继任者一个“全新的开始”，而拖延到年底将阻碍董事会选出的任何一位领导公司的人。

paragraphs.en: For more than a year, intrigue has swirled over who will get the top job, whether it will be an insider or an outsider, and how the company plans to manage the transition.
paragraphs.cn: 一年多来，关于谁将获得最高职位、是内部人还是外部人，以及公司计划如何管理过渡期，一直充满着各种猜测。

paragraphs.en: In February of 2020, Iger stepped down as CEO for the first time after a 15-year stint, handing the reins to Bob Chapek, who headed the theme parks division and had become a top deputy to Iger. Iger stayed on for more than a year and half as executive chairman, to advise Chapek and smooth the transition.
paragraphs.cn: 2020年2月，艾格在担任CEO 15年后首次卸任，将大权交给了鲍勃·查佩克（Bob Chapek），后者曾领导主题公园部门，并成为艾格的首席副手。艾格以执行董事长身份留任一年半多，为查佩克提供建议并平稳过渡。

paragraphs.en: As the Covid-19 pandemic unfolded, however, the two men clashed frequently and intensely, leading to a chaotic period for the 100-year-old entertainment giant that culminated in the board ousting Chapek in November 2022. The company brought back Iger for a second stint as CEO.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，随着新冠疫情的爆发，两人频繁而激烈地发生冲突，导致这家百年娱乐巨头经历了一段混乱时期，最终董事会于2022年11月罢免了查佩克。公司随后请回艾格，让他第二次担任CEO。

paragraphs.en: The board, chaired by former Morgan Stanley CEO James Gorman, has carefully orchestrated the selection process while also keeping it under tight wraps. That is a marked contrast from 2020, when Iger was board chairman and drove the decision to name Chapek as his successor.
paragraphs.cn: 由摩根士丹利前CEO詹姆斯·戈尔曼（James Gorman）担任主席的董事会，精心策划了遴选过程，同时严格保密。这与2020年形成了鲜明对比，当时艾格是董事会主席，并主导了任命查佩克为继任者的决定。

paragraphs.en: In a recent public filing, Disney’s board said it would make its decision in the current quarter and that internal candidates are receiving mentoring from Iger and external coaches.
paragraphs.cn: 在最近的一份公开文件中，迪士尼董事会表示将在本季度做出决定，并且内部候选人正在接受艾格和外部教练的指导。

paragraphs.en: Appeared in the January 31, 2026, print edition as 'Iger Eyes Earlier Exit From CEO Role At Disney'.
paragraphs.cn: 刊登于2026年1月31日印刷版，标题为“艾格考虑提前卸任迪士尼CEO职务”。

paragraphs.en: Robbie Whelan covers large tech companies for the Wall Street Journal, with a focus on semiconductor giants Nvidia, AMD, Intel, Broadcom and Qualcomm.
paragraphs.cn: 罗比·惠兰（Robbie Whelan）是《华尔街日报》的记者，负责报道大型科技公司，重点关注半导体巨头英伟达、AMD、英特尔、博通和高通。

paragraphs.en: A WSJ reporter since 2010, Robbie previously covered the business of Hollywood, public health policy, shipping companies, the housing market and commercial real estate. From 2016 to 2020, he was a Latin America correspondent covering politics, trade, economics, immigration and the drug war. He is the author of the forthcoming book "The House of Mouse: Bob Iger and the Fight for the Soul of Disney."
paragraphs.cn: 罗比自2010年起担任《华尔街日报》记者，此前曾报道好莱坞商业、公共卫生政策、航运公司、房地产市场和商业地产。2016年至2020年，他担任拉丁美洲记者，报道政治、贸易、经济、移民和毒品战争。他是即将出版的《鼠屋：鲍勃·艾格与迪士尼灵魂之战》一书的作者。

paragraphs.en: Robbie grew up in Pittsburgh and studied history at Johns Hopkins University. He's a massive Steelers fan, a music obsessive, and has played mandolin and guitar in bluegrass and country-rock bands in Brooklyn, Mexico City, Baltimore and Pittsburgh. He lives in Los Angeles.
paragraphs.cn: 罗比在匹兹堡长大，在约翰霍普金斯大学学习历史。他是一名狂热的钢人队球迷，痴迷音乐，曾在布鲁克林、墨西哥城、巴尔的摩和匹兹堡的蓝草和乡村摇滚乐队中演奏曼陀林和吉他。他住在洛杉矶。

paragraphs.en: Jessica Toonkel covers the biggest corporate developments in media, entertainment and tech. She joined The Wall Street Journal from the Information, where she broke many stories, including Amazon's acquisition of MGM and the New York Times' buyout of the Athletic as well as Disney's plans for an ad-supported streaming service.
paragraphs.cn: 杰西卡·图恩克尔（Jessica Toonkel）负责报道媒体、娱乐和科技领域最重要的企业发展。她从The Information加入《华尔街日报》，在那里她报道了许多独家新闻，包括亚马逊收购米高梅、纽约时报收购The Athletic以及迪士尼推出广告支持流媒体服务的计划。

paragraphs.en: Prior to her four years at the Information, Jessica spent seven years at Reuters, where she consistently broke news on major deals, executive changes and strategic developments in media, tech and finance. Earlier in her career, she covered funds and investing for publications such as InvestmentNews, American Banker and Institutional Investor.
paragraphs.cn: 在The Information工作四年之前，杰西卡在路透社工作了七年，期间她持续报道媒体、科技和金融领域的重要交易、高管变动和战略发展。职业生涯早期，她曾为InvestmentNews、American Banker和Institutional Investor等刊物报道基金和投资。

paragraphs.en: Ben Fritz covers the entertainment industry from The Wall Street Journal's Los Angeles bureau. He was previously the Journal's artificial intelligence editor and its West Coast U.S. News editor. Before joining the Journal in 2013, Ben covered Hollywood for the Los Angeles Times and Variety.
paragraphs.cn: 本·弗里茨（Ben Fritz）在《华尔街日报》洛杉矶分社报道娱乐业。他曾担任该报的人工智能编辑和美国西海岸新闻编辑。在2013年加入《华尔街日报》之前，本曾为《洛杉矶时报》和《综艺》杂志报道好莱坞。

paragraphs.en: He reported and hosted The Journal podcast miniseries "With Great Power: The Rise of Superhero Cinema" and is author of the bestselling books "The Big Picture: The Fight for the Future of Movies" and "All the President's Spin."
paragraphs.cn: 他报道并主持了《华尔街日报》播客迷你剧“权力越大：超级英雄电影的崛起”，并且是畅销书《大局：电影未来的斗争》和《总统的谎言》的作者。

paragraphs.en: Ben has won awards from the Los Angeles Press Club and the Society for Advanced Business Editing and Writing and edited coverage that won awards from the National Association of Black Journalists and the New York Press Club.
paragraphs.cn: 本曾获得洛杉矶新闻俱乐部和高级商业编辑与写作协会的奖项，并编辑过获得全国黑人记者协会和纽约新闻俱乐部奖项的报道。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Colleagues or partners in a business or profession.

vocabulary.meaning_en: A written or spoken agreement, especially one concerning employment, sales, or tenancy, that is intended to be enforceable by law.
vocabulary.example: Chief Executive Bob Iger has told associates that he plans to step down as CEO and pull back from daily management before the Dec. 31 end of his contract, according to people familiar with the matter.
vocabulary.example_cn: 首席执行官鲍勃·艾格已告知同事，他计划在合同于12月31日到期前辞去CEO职务，并退出日常管理。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A group of people who manage or supervise the affairs of a company or corporation.
vocabulary.example: The entertainment giant’s board of directors is planning to meet next week at its headquarters in Burbank, Calif., where they are expected to vote on who should take the top job, according to people familiar with the matter.
vocabulary.example_cn: 据知情人士透露，这家娱乐巨头的董事会计划下周在加利福尼亚州伯班克的总部召开会议，预计届时将投票决定谁将接任最高职位。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The main administrative center of an organization.
vocabulary.example: The entertainment giant’s board of directors is planning to meet next week at its headquarters in Burbank, Calif., where they are expected to vote on who should take the top job, according to people familiar with the matter.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这家娱乐巨头的董事会计划下周在加利福尼亚州伯班克的总部召开会议，预计届时将投票决定谁将接任最高职位。

vocabulary.meaning_en: To give all or a large part of one's time or resources to (a person, activity, or cause).
vocabulary.example: He has said he would also like to devote more time to work with his wife, Willow Bay, dean of the USC Annenberg School for Communication and Journalism, and on Angel City FC, the women’s soccer team they bought in 2024.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他还表示，希望投入更多时间与妻子、南加州大学安纳伯格传播与新闻学院院长柳·贝（Willow Bay）一起工作，并参与他们于2024年收购的女子足球队天使城FC的事务。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The action of leaving, especially to start a journey. In a business context, leaving a position.
vocabulary.example: The final timing of his departure from the top job hasn’t yet been determined and could change.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他卸任最高职位的最终时间尚未确定，并可能发生变化。

vocabulary.meaning_en: To take over a throne, inheritance, office, or other position from someone else.
vocabulary.example: Iger is expected to remain CEO for several months after the company announces who will succeed him so that he can mentor the new leader, people with knowledge of his plans said.
vocabulary.example_cn: 据了解他计划的人士称，艾格预计将在公司宣布谁将继任他之后继续担任CEO数月，以便指导新领导人。

vocabulary.meaning_en: To advise or train someone, especially a younger colleague.
vocabulary.example: Iger is expected to remain CEO for several months after the company announces who will succeed him so that he can mentor the new leader, people with knowledge of his plans said.
vocabulary.example_cn: 艾格预计将在公司宣布继任者后继续担任CEO数月，以便指导新领导人。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A person who applies for a job or is nominated for election.
vocabulary.example: People close to the company believe the CEO race has come down to two candidates: Experiences chairman Josh D’Amaro, who oversees theme parks, consumer products and videogames, and Dana Walden, a veteran television executive who co-chairs Disney’s entertainment business, including streaming.
vocabulary.example_cn: 接近公司的人士认为，CEO之争已归结为两名候选人：负责主题公园、消费产品和视频游戏的体验部门主席乔什·达马罗（Josh D’Amaro），以及共同领导迪士尼娱乐业务（包括流媒体）的资深电视高管达娜·瓦尔登（Dana Walden）。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The process or a period of changing from one state or condition to another. In business, often refers to changing leadership.
vocabulary.example: He could retain a role on the board and at the company after the CEO transition.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在CEO过渡后，他可能会在董事会和公司中保留一个职位。
